items.original_text: RT Arthur MacWaters I called my engineer friend at SpaceX the other day. 1am Pacific. 3am in Starbase. He was still in the office and not going home any time soon. And he was happy. Energized. Because he's directly influencing the course of a multi-planetary future for humanity. On a fundamental level, this is why Elon companies win. Young, high-competence people are given exceptional individual agency and semi-impossible problems. And they know that their contribution is essential. Any company that does this is much more likely to succeed. Elon does this without fail. Inspires me a lot. David Senra: Marc Andreessen (@pmarca) on what it’s like to work at SpaceX: “It’s like being dropped into a zone of shocking competence.” “Everybody is ultra competent, and the reason everybody’s ultra competent is because if they’re not, Elon sniffs it out and fires them. He knows, ‘cause
items.zh_text: 转推 Arthur MacWaters：前几天我给我在 SpaceX 的工程师朋友打了个电话。太平洋时间凌晨 1 点，星基地时间是凌晨 3 点。他还在办公室，短时间内不打算回家。而且他很开心，充满干劲。因为他正直接影响人类多行星未来的进程。从根本上说，这就是埃隆的公司能赢的原因。年轻、高能力的人被赋予非凡的个人自主权和半不可能解决的问题。他们知道自己的贡献至关重要。任何这样做的公司都更有可能成功。埃隆始终如一地做到这一点。这让我深受启发。David Senra：马克·安德森（@pmarca）谈在 SpaceX 工作的感受：“就像被扔进一个令人震惊的能力区。”“每个人都极其能干，而每个人极其能干的原因是，如果他们不是，埃隆会嗅出来并解雇他们。他知道，因为

items.original_text: It is the answer Nic Cruz Patane: This was my favorite moment from Elon, Franz, and Lars yesterday. Elon: "I’ll tell you something, maybe at a fundamental level why the Model S/X succeeded, which is that, those cars were designed with love." Lars: "Absolutely." Elon: "That would be my advice for people out
items.zh_text: 这是尼克·克鲁兹·帕塔内的答案：这是昨天我最喜欢的埃隆、弗朗茨和拉尔斯之间的时刻。埃隆：‘我跟你说件事，也许从根本上解释了为什么Model S/X成功了，那就是，这些车是用爱设计的。’拉尔斯：‘绝对没错。’埃隆：‘这就是我给外界的建议。’

items.original_text: Try it out! Michael Truell: For the next month, we’re doubling Cursor usage for new users you invite on the Teams plan. Enjoy!
items.zh_text: 试试看！Michael Truell：接下来一个月，对于你在Teams计划中邀请的新用户，我们将Cursor的使用量翻倍。尽情享受吧！
